title: 2025 OMJ Facebook Analytics Dashboard
summary: Built a full-year Excel dashboard/report organizing Pike County OMJ Facebook page metrics, charts, audience data, and top content into a navigable analytics workbook.
goal: Make a full calendar year of Facebook page analytics easier to review, navigate, and reference by organizing key numbers, embedded graphs, audience information, and top content breakdowns into a structured Excel report.
role: Built the Excel dashboard/report structure for the full 2025 calendar year.
Organized Facebook page metrics into clear sections for year totals, top content, and audience breakdowns.
Used Facebook-provided numbers and graphs to make performance trends easier to navigate and compare.
Highlighted major metrics such as views, content interactions, follower count, watch time, and top-performing posts.

title: Spring 2026 Newsletter — The Competitive Edge
summary: Refreshed and produced the Spring 2026 edition of *The Competitive Edge*, a 12-page Workforce & Business Development newsletter distributed through Constant Contact and published on the program website.
goal: Create a professional, easy-to-read newsletter that gathered approved success stories, program updates, event promotions, and calls to action into one public-facing publication.
role: Updated and freshened up an inherited cover-page template.
Gathered approved success stories from coworkers and organized them in Canva.
Wrote the “Message from Director” on the cover page.
Wrote the full Quiktrip Transportation LLC article on page 3.
Wrote the full Double Dog Dari Bar article on page 5.
Adapted approved content into an article for the Car Repair Program section.
Designed both event flyers that appeared on pages 10 and 11.
Built the final page from the ground up as a refreshed social/web call-to-action page.
Used Constant Contact and Wix to send the newsletter and upload it for website viewers.
process: Collected approved program/client content from coworkers.
Imported and arranged copy into Canva for readability and visual consistency.
Balanced long-form articles, success stories, flyers, and calls to action across a 12-page format.
Prepared the newsletter for public distribution through email and website channels.

title: Business Development Program Video Advert
summary: Created a short-form Facebook video advertisement for the Business Development Program, designed to be minimal, informative, and attention-grabbing for social media scrollers.
goal: Quickly communicate the program’s value, hold attention long enough to deliver the core message, and end with clear contact information.
role: Created the video advert solo.
Built the video using Canva clips and overlays.
Used royalty-free clips and subtle background music.
Generated the AI voiceover in Clipchamp.
Timed the video clips around the voiceover audio.
Timed captions to match the voiceover and pacing.
Used approved/revised wording from the program director.
process: Started with a minimal, scroller-friendly concept.
Selected royalty-free visuals that fit the business development message.
Created voiceover first, then structured timing, clips, captions, and pacing around the audio.
Ended with contact information and a direct program call to action.

title: Small Business Animated Social Media Post
summary: Designed an animated Facebook post for the Business Development Program to promote no-cost planning support for small businesses.
goal: Create a professional, brand-conscious animated asset that avoided visual clutter while still catching attention in the social feed.
role: Designed the animated post for Facebook release.
Sourced stock footage.
Built the animated graphic in Canva.
Focused on quick readability, clean layout, and a clear small-business support message.
Paired the post with a separately approved caption, not included in the reviewed files.
process: Kept the layout intentionally minimal.
Used animation and stock footage to add movement without making the post feel busy.
Designed around fast-read messaging for social media viewers.

title: Business Summit 2026 Outbound Email
summary: Built and sent a Constant Contact email campaign promoting Business Summit 2026: “Beyond the Background — Potential Over Past.”
goal: Promote the event to employers and business leaders with clear details, registration information, partner branding, and compliant email structure.
role: Created and sent the campaign in Constant Contact.
Adapted selected sections from past campaigns for consistency.
Updated logos and event/body text for the new summit.
Preserved compliance-oriented footer sections such as unsubscribe information.
process: Used an existing campaign structure where it made sense for consistency and compliance.
Reworked the email around the new event theme, speakers/topics, registration details, and partner branding.
Prepared the campaign for public distribution through Constant Contact.

title: Canva Workshop Flyer
summary: Created a Canva Workshop flyer to promote an in-person training session developed from scratch.
goal: Advertise a hands-on Canva class and explain how attendees could register.
role: Developed the workshop/lesson from scratch.
Designed the flyer in Canva.
Structured the class around in-person examples, hands-on work/help, and Q&A.
Used the flyer to communicate topics, location, time, and registration contact information.
process: Built the lesson around practical Canva use cases.
Designed a high-visibility flyer with clear topic bullets and registration instructions.
Positioned the class as approachable for attendees with basic computer skills.

title: OMJ Commercial / Lobby Loop Video
summary: Collaborated with a mentor on a major update to an OhioMeansJobs lobby-loop commercial showcasing Community Action Committee of Pike County services.
goal: Refresh an existing lobby video so clients could see current, readable, visually engaging information about multiple Community Action programs.
role: Collaborated with mentor on the major update.
Updated, replaced, or removed outdated information.
Preserved useful order/structure from the original version.
Sourced topic-specific stock footage for section backgrounds.
Animated screen elements.
Refreshed graphics and layouts.
Used high-contrast text colors for readability in a lobby/display environment.
Completed an approved version that is still in use today.
process: Started from an existing core video made by mentor.
Identified information that needed removed, replaced, or modernized.
Kept the useful structure while updating visual treatment, footage, animation, and readability.
Designed with looping lobby playback in mind.

title: OMJ Van Event Advert Template
type: Event Video Advert / Reusable Social Template
summary: Created a reusable video advert concept for recurring OhioMeansJobs van events, transforming a static monthly flyer workflow into a more engaging social media promotion.
goal: Bring recurring OMJ van event flyers to life while keeping the template easy for a coworker to update with new date, time, and location details.
role: Designed a branded static layout in Canva with logos along the top and event explanation/details along the right side.
Shot all event footage personally using a camera gimbal.
Included the OMJ van and outreach table setup in the footage.
Edited the full video in Canva and Clipchamp.
Applied a desaturated-to-vivid color shift timed to the music.
Used fades, morph transitions, outward pan/spiral motion, close-up table pans, wide shots, and sideways pan footage from another event.
Added animated on-screen graphics promoting Facebook and the website.
Designed the file as a reusable template for future monthly event promotion.
process: Identified a recurring static flyer workflow that could be improved.
Created an editable visual structure so future event details could be quickly swapped.
Captured original event footage to make the asset feel grounded and real.
Synced motion, color, and transitions to the music for a more polished social video.

title: Business Summit 2026 Welcome Banner
summary: Designed a large-format welcome banner for Business Summit 2026, incorporating logos from the multiple groups and partners involved in the event.
goal: Create a clean, clear, readable welcome banner for display on the wall during the Business Summit.
role: Designed the welcome banner in Canva.
Included logos for all different groups involved in the event.
Focused on wall-display readability and clean output.
Used HP Print to print the banner on the organization’s banner printer.
process: Balanced event title/theme with many partner logos.
Prioritized readability at distance for a physical event environment.
Prepared and produced the final banner through in-house print equipment.
